title: Was Shinjis Contemplation unique
wikitext:
[]: Was Shinji's "Contemplation" unique?             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Jul 22, 2010 8:12 pm Was Shinji's time spent as LCL in EoE unique? Did he really hold the fate of humanity in his hands, and the entire world as we know it rested solely on his decision? I don't think so, and this also ties in with the time elapsed in the final scene, as if everyone experienced the same thing, Asuka could have gotten there before Shinji, and erected the graves while he was still unconscious. Unless the fact that Shinji was in Unit 01 while it happened, and that Unit 01 "killed" the Mega-Rei means that it really was his decision for all of humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Jul 22, 2010 8:40 pm It was his decision. For whatever reason Rei decided to give him final say over anyone else.

[]: Re: Was Shinji's "Contemplation" unique?             Quote Post by Tycho » Thu Jul 22, 2010 9:40 pm BeoX2 wrote: Was Shinji's time spent as LCL in EoE unique? Did he really hold the fate of humanity in his hands, and the entire world as we know it rested solely on his decision? I don't think so, and this also ties in with the time elapsed in the final scene, as if everyone experienced the same thing, Asuka could have gotten there before Shinji, and erected the graves while he was still unconscious. Unless the fact that Shinji was in Unit 01 while it happened, and that Unit 01 "killed" the Mega-Rei means that it really was his decision for all of humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:49 am Rei was given control over Third Impact during her ascension to Godhood. Her decision on what to do? Ask Shinji what he wanted.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Fri Jul 23, 2010 8:08 am the way i saw it, everybody got to experience that sort of mind-melding, everybody-knows-what-everybody-thinks, everybody-feels-what-others-feel experience, but they probably didn't have the comfort of Rei being there to explain what the heck was going on. IMO Shinji was unique in that aspect, as the decision rested on him-- and it's also why the was the first to come out... he was told point-blank that he could. i can imagine it took the rest of the very confused consciousnesses a bit more time to figure it out.

[]:             Quote Post by Velefor » Fri Jul 23, 2010 8:53 am Indeed Shinji's state during instrumentality was unique, at least in EoE. The main distinction between him and others is that his mind was never fully connected to everybody else on the planet, and probably maintained the highest individuality at that point while others where disappearing and merging. It’s not clear whether or not he was turned into LCL, so probably he was because how else could his body reappeared in that beach.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:02 am As far as EoE is concerned, it seems that only those Rei selected were involved in the interpersonal stuff of Pre-instrumentality. Once the main event kicked off everyone who wasn't Shinji, Rei or Kaworu (or Yui?) got sucked into one big psychospiritual pool and quickly stopped existing in a form that is capable of having experiences of any sort.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jul 23, 2010 11:02 am NemZ wrote: As far as EoE is concerned, it seems that only those Rei selected were involved in the interpersonal stuff of Pre-instrumentality. Once the main event kicked off everyone who wasn't Shinji, Rei or Kaworu (or Yui?) got sucked into one big psychospiritual pool and quickly stopped existing in a form that is capable of having experiences of any sort.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 12:47 pm Azathoth wrote: Yui is in there for certain, and I think you could make a case for Misato and Asuka, too; Asuka certainly seems to have experienced Yui's farewell to Shinji, at least.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Jul 24, 2010 5:59 pm NemZ wrote: Asuka's gesture reminding Shinji of Yui is intentional on Anno's part, but why should we assume Asuka knows it's significance?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 24, 2010 6:26 pm NemZ wrote: it's significance?

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sat Jul 24, 2010 6:32 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Being a fond farewell, you mean?
